evals.name: mcp-server-tutorial
evals.prompt: Write a technical blog post about how to build an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server. Make it engaging and practical, with code examples and a conversational tone. Target audience is developers who want to extend AI tools.
evals.expected_output: A blog post with: relatable hook about why MCP matters, practical code examples early, conversational tone with 'I' and 'you', tables showing commands/configs, expert quotes if relevant, personal anecdotes, and a CTA at the end

evals.name: agent-architecture-guide
evals.prompt: Create an architecture documentation piece explaining the difference between ReAct agents and long-horizon agents. Use the engaging, scenario-driven writing style. Include practical implementation tips.
evals.expected_output: Architecture doc with: story-driven intro about agent evolution, visual comparisons (tables/diagrams), practical implementation code, personal opinions on tradeoffs, expert validation, and community CTA

evals.name: prompt-engineering-tips
evals.prompt: Write a tutorial on advanced prompt engineering techniques for Claude. Make it fun and practical - start with immediate value, use the conversational blogger style, include real prompt examples.
evals.expected_output: Tutorial with: catchy hook about prompt engineering struggles, table of ready-to-use prompts upfront, explanation of why they work, code examples, personal tips, and engaging conclusion with next steps
